summary: Single clamiga-side root cause for both symptoms: the per-thread top-level ABORT restart auto-established in src/core/builtins_thread.c thread_entry() is created with report = CL_NIL (cl_make_restart(SYM_ABORT, abort_handler, CL_NIL, ...) at ~line 162). The printer's TYPE_RESTART case (printer.c:1224-1271) prints a restart's description for PRINC/~A only when report is non-NIL, otherwise falling back to the escaped #<RESTART NAME> form (intentional for report-less user restarts, tested at test_condition.c:666). SLY/slynk princ's the restart to list SLDB restarts and derives the abort reason from it, so a NIL report surfaces as "#<RESTART ABORT>" (bug 1) and a NIL/"NIL" abort reason (bug 2). Fix: give the auto-established ABORT restart a "Return to top level" report string (matching clamiga's own debugger wording at debugger.c:130) via a cached lazy-init helper modeled on the existing get_thread_abort_handler(). clamiga's interactive debugger (debugger.c display_restarts) lists restarts by NAME only and always appends a literal "Return to top level" line, so the #<RESTART ABORT> text cannot originate there — it is the SLY/slynk princ path.
affected_areas: Restart construction: src/core/builtins_thread.c (per-thread top-level ABORT restart — add a cached report string and pass it to cl_make_restart)
Restart printing: src/core/printer.c TYPE_RESTART case (read-only confirmation; the report-less fallback is correct and must NOT change)
Allocation: src/core/mem.c cl_make_restart (read-only; already GC-protects the report arg)
Test suites: tests/test_threads.c (host worker-thread find-restart + princ-to-string) and tests/amiga/run-tests.lisp (Amiga)
External integration (not in repo): SLY/slynk SLDB restart listing and (:abort reason) derivation — verification target only
risks: Bug 2 (throw-to-toplevel returning (:abort "NIL")) is mediated by SLY's slynk, which is loaded externally and is NOT present in this repository, so it cannot be reproduced or regression-tested by clamiga's host or Amiga suites. The clamiga lever is the same NIL report on the auto-established ABORT restart, but the exact (:abort reason) value depends on slynk's reason-derivation. If slynk derives the reason from the signaled condition (NIL on a bare throw-to-toplevel) rather than from the restart's report, the report fix alone may not change the "NIL" reason; confirm by running ./build/host/clamiga under SLY in a verification step.

The fix only touches the per-thread top-level ABORT restart established in builtins_thread.c thread_entry. The main/initial thread has no auto-established ABORT restart (repl.c establishes none); slynk runs evaluations in worker threads via mp:make-thread, so the thread-top restart is the one surfaced. Aborts attempted on the main thread remain without an abort restart (pre-existing behavior, out of scope).

The host regression test relies on find-restart 'abort resolving the thread-top ABORT restart from inside a worker thread; if cl_restart_floor excludes it, use (car (last (compute-restarts))) instead (the thread-top ABORT restart is the outermost binding).

Do not alter the printer's report-less fallback (#<RESTART NAME>) — it is depended on by tests/test_condition.c:666 (lisp_restart_no_report_princ) and is correct CLHS behavior for user restarts defined without a :report.

The cached report string is shared (read-only) across all worker threads' ABORT restarts; this is safe (immutable strings, printer reads only) and is created lazily on first worker thread exactly like the existing thread_abort_handler.
